name: Pepparkakor/Martha White
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the butter and shortening with the sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Add the eggs and molasses; beat well to combine.
3. Sift together the flour and remaining ingredients (baking soda, ginger, cinnamon, ground cloves, nutmeg, and salt).
4. Blend the sifted dry ingredients into the creamed mixture.
5. Cover and refrigerate for 1 hour and 30 minutes.
6. Divide the dough into three equal parts.
7. Shape each part into a roll measuring 6 1/2 x 1 1/2 inches.
8. Wrap each roll in waxed paper, sealing the ends securely.
9. Chill the rolls for 3 hours or overnight.
10. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
11. Cut the chilled rolls into slices that are 1/8 inch thick.
12. Arrange the slices one inch apart on an ungreased baking sheet.
13. Bake for 6 to 8 minutes, or until they are a deep golden brown color.
14. Transfer the cookies to wire racks to cool completely.
